questions.question: In the following questions, a sentence has been given in Direct/ Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Direct/ Indirect speech. "We will give him the best directions we can," replied the damsels.
options: The damsels replied that they would give him the best directions they can.
The damsels replied that they will give him the best directions they can.
The damsels replied that they would give him the best directions they could.
The damsels replied that they would gave him the best directions they could.
questions.explanation: The simple future tense 'will give' will change to the future condition 'would give', when converting direct to indirect speech. Additionally, the modal verb 'can' will change to 'could'. C is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given with two words in bold. From the options below, choose the combination of words that replace the highlighted words to make the sentence grammatically and meaningfully correct. In case the highlighted words are correct as they are, choose 'No Improvement' as your answer. Near every cell in the human body contains 23 pairs of chromosomes, tinier packages of DNA and proteins nestled in the cell's nucleus.
questions.explanation: An adverb ('nearly') would qualify/modify the word 'every'; 'tiny' would be correct usage here since no comparison is made in the given statement, therefore, there is no use of comparative degree. Option B satisfies the conditions mentioned above; hence, B is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions below, a sentence is given with two words in bold. From the options below, choose the combination of words that replace the highlighted words to make the sentence grammatically and meaningfully correct. In case the highlighted words are correct as they are, choose 'No Improvement' as your answer. You could also trying to negotiate different work arrangements or schedules or requested a move to a different office.
questions.explanation: Continuous form of the verb 'try' is incorrect; 'try' would be correct here. Since the statement is in present tense, 'requested' would be incorrect here; 'request' would be correct. Hence, C is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Rapacious
questions.explanation: Rapacious- aggressively greedy or grasping. Avaricious - having or showing an extreme greed for wealth or material gain. Magnanimous- generous or forgiving, especially towards a rival or less powerful person. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which best expresses the meaning of the given word. Incursion
questions.explanation: Incursion- an invasion or attack, especially a sudden or brief one. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Concede
questions.explanation: Concede- admit or agree that something is true after first denying or resisting it; surrender or yield. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, choose the word opposite in meaning to the given word. Saddle
questions.explanation: Saddle- burden (someone) with an onerous responsibility or task. Relieve - free someone from (a tiresome responsibility). Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, four words are given, out of which only one word is incorrectly spelled. Find the incorrectly spelled word.
questions.explanation: D is incorrect. The correct spelling is "enterprise" which means "a business or company." Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, four words are given, out of which only one word is incorrectly spelled. Find the incorrectly spelled word.
questions.explanation: B is incorrect. The correct spelling is "cognitive" which means "connected with thinking or conscious mental processes." Option B is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the sentence, identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. It is also easy to overlook how profoundly an ethical dilemma mass repression can pose.
questions.explanation: D is incorrect. An adjective modifies a noun or a noun phrase. The adverb 'profoundly' is incorrect here and the adjective 'profound' should instead be used to correctly modify the noun phrase 'an ethical dilemma'.

questions.question: In the sentence, identify the segment which contains the grammatical error. In our universe, time seems to go from past to future, not of reverse.
questions.explanation: D is incorrect. The preposition 'of' is grammatically incorrect here. 'Of' is a preposition that indicates relationships between other words, such as belonging, things made of other things, things that contain other things, or a point of reckoning. The preposition 'in' has numerous uses and it is the correct word to be used here.

questions.question: A
questions.explanation: Since the required word is preceded by an adjective, we are looking for a noun. The words in options A, C and D are adjective, and are hence ruled out, as there is no noun that follows (A). 'Perspective' (outlook/aspect) is the right word, which also fits in the context ('environmental perspective') which talks different benefits of expanding renewable energy capacity. Option B is the right answer.

questions.question: B
questions.explanation: The sentence poses a question, asking whether the government's efforts to take advantage of favourable cost conditions on renewable energy are sufficient. Since it lists two types of renewable energy in particular, 'especially' is the most suitable word here. The other options do not fit in the context. Hysterical - Affected by or deriving from wildly uncontrolled emotion. Option D is the right answer.

questions.question: C
questions.explanation: The sentence tells us that China and Brazil have done much better in the renewable energy scene, compared to India. Since the previous sentence mentions that the costs of electricity generation from renewable sources have declined, 'advantage' is the suitable word here. The other words fail to make the sentence meaningful. Surrogacy - The process of giving birth as a surrogate mother or of arranging such a birth. Option B is the right answer.

questions.question: D
questions.explanation: The sentence tells us that costs of renewable energy have reduced. Also, the use of 'despite' indicates an element of contrast. 'Reap' (obtain) is the suitable word here, to indicate where India is lacking. A and B do not fit contextually. D does not fit grammatically, as we need a verb in its root form, due to the presence of 'to'. Option C is the right answer. Dislodge - remove.

questions.question: E
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about certain 'duty' i.e. tax on imported solar cells, which has lessened the cost advantage of renewable energy. 'Imposition' is the suitable word in this context. Imposition - Require (a duty, charge, or penalty) to be undertaken or paid. Exposition - A comprehensive description and explanation of an idea or theory. Supposition - A belief held without proof or certain knowledge; an assumption or hypothesis. Reposition - Place in a different position; adjust or alter the position of. Option A is the right answer.

questions.question: In each of the questions given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by 4 options, each having a word which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word that fits in the blank. Robinson, famed for his Christian beliefs and his ___________ lifestyle, also found time to have lunch with his mother, who still lives in Leeds.
questions.explanation: The sentence given above talks about a person's beliefs, his lifestyle and his place of dwelling. The blank requires an adjective that can define his lifestyle. The options present before us have only two adjectives 'acrid' (unpleasantly bitter or pungent) and 'abstemious' (austere/self-disciplined); the former is not the right adjective to describe one's lifestyle (it cannot be bitter or pungent); the latter is correct and would refer to the person's disciplined lifestyle. 'Acreage' (dimensions of an area of land) is a noun and 'abash' (make (someone) feel embarrassed, disconcerted, or ashamed) a verb; hence, are grammatically inappropriate. Options a), b) and c) are eliminated. The answer is option d).

questions.question: In each of the questions given below, a sentence is given with one blank, followed by 4 options, each having a word which may or may not fit in the blank. From the given options, choose the one that gives the correct word that fits in the blank. Just prior to the onset of America's involvement in World War II in 1941, President Franklin D. Roosevelt sought to __________ several service associations into one organization to lift the morale of the military and nourish support on the home front.
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about the American President Franklin D. Roosevelt's efforts to lift the morale of the military. Since, the blank is preceded by the infinitive 'to', it will take the base form of verb; 'coalesce' which means 'unite or join' is hence the right word for the blank. 'Grotesque' (1. Distorted, 2. outrageous) and 'afire' (on fire) are adjectives ('grotesque' is a noun too); 'knelled' (ring a bell) is not the base form of verb; therefore, the options b), c) and d) are eliminated. Option a) is the answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, a passage is split into 4 parts and named as P, Q, R and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the passage and find out which of the four combinations/sequence is correct. P. The terms include funding a large part of the discounts, using only the aggregators' fleet to fulfil delivery, a drastic reduction in meal preparation time, and complete non-transparency over how in-app recommendations work and what restaurants can do to get recommended more often. Q. This, they added, is helping the aggregator build a vast database of consumers' dining habits and even help expand their own business in what can be seen as a conflict of interest by restaurants. R. Restaurants, both big and small, are increasingly finding themselves being coerced into accepting terms and conditions favourable to online aggregators. S. Then, there are other concerns with regard to customer data - some restaurant owners claimed that some large aggregators refuse to share data on who their customers are.
questions.explanation: The correct order is RPSQ. Only statement R can be the first sentence of the paragraph as rest of the sentences start with an ambiguity (this, then, the terms). Statement R introduces the topic of discussion i.e., restaurants forced into accepting the terms of online aggregators. R is then followed by the statement P which gives further details about the terms and conditions imposed by the aggregators on restaurants and the non-transparency by aggregators over the in-app recommendations. S will follow P (notice that the sentence mentions 'other' concerns which indicates that S will follow P and not the other way around) which states the refusal by the aggregators to share data about the customers. Q follows S as it explains why the aggregators are refusing to share customer data i.e., to build a database of dining habits of customers and expand their own business. The right answer is option b).

questions.question: In the following questions, a passage is split into 4 parts and named as P, Q, R and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the passage and find out which of the four combinations/sequence is correct. P. But the objective is to take India to $80-90 billion by 2025, said Dr Omkar Rai, director general, Software Technology Park of India (STPI). Q. Dr. Rai was speaking at the 15th India Innovation Summit 2019, organised by the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) in Bengaluru. R. The two-day summit aims to create a stage for industry captains, academia technocrats, young innovators, and policy makers to participate and deliberate on the nuances of innovation. S. The global software product industry is currently valued at $511 billion, of which India's share is $8.1 billion.
questions.explanation: The correct order is SPQR. Only S can be the first statement because it is the only independent statement in the paragraph and introduces the topic of discussion, i.e., the global software industry and its valuation. S is followed by P because it further states the goals/objective set by India. Statement P mentions Dr. Rai, the director general of STPI and statement P is followed by Q as it reveals that Dr. Rai was delivering the aforementioned statements at the Confederation of Indian Industry in Bengaluru. Statement Q is followed by R as it further gives details about the summit and its objectives. Option c) is the right answer.

questions.question: In each the following questions, out of the four given options, select the one which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Cost a bomb
questions.explanation: Cost a bomb - extremely expensive. So, option D is the right answer. "The renovation of the city hall cost a bomb."

questions.question: In each the following questions, out of the four given options, select the one which best expresses the meaning of the idiom/phrase. Go through the roof
questions.explanation: Go through the roof - become very angry. It can also mean '(of prices or figures) reach extreme or unexpected heights'. We can see that option B is the right answer here. "When I was expelled from school, my parents went through the roof."

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. Still in existence
questions.explanation: Fictitious- not real or true; imaginary or fabricated; unreal. Genuine- authentic and sincere. Extant- still in existence; surviving. Option A is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. A person who supports an idea or theory and tries to persuade people of its truth or benefits
questions.explanation: Practitioner- a person actively engaged in an art, discipline, or profession, especially medicine. Bureaucrat - an official in a government department, in particular one perceived as being concerned with procedural correctness at the expense of people's needs. Exponent - a person who supports an idea or belief or performs an activity. Critic - someone whose job is to give their opinion about something, especially films, books, music, etc. Option D is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the below questions, a sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice. The General was followed into the room by his younger daughter.
options: The General's younger daughter follows him into the room.
The General's younger daughter had followed him into the room.
His younger daughter followed the General into the room.
The General's younger daughter followed him into the room.
questions.explanation: Option D is the right answer. Option A is eliminated as it uses the present tense, while the original sentence has used the simple past tense. Option B is eliminated as uses the past perfect tense, while the original sentence has not done so. Option C is eliminated as it fails to specify whose daughter followed the General to the room, while the original sentence clearly does so.
